root_text.an11.6:1.1: “Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkosako paribhāsako ariyūpavādo sabrahmacārīnaṁ, ṭhānametaṁ avakāso yaṁ so ekādasannaṁ byasanānaṁ aññataraṁ byasanaṁ nigaccheyya.
root_text.an11.6:2.12: kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati—
root_text.an11.6:2.13: yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkosako paribhāsako ariyūpavādo sabrahmacārīnaṁ, ṭhānametaṁ avakāso yaṁ so imesaṁ ekādasannaṁ byasanānaṁ aññataraṁ byasanaṁ nigaccheyya.
root_text.an11.6:3.1: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkosako paribhāsako ariyūpavādo sabrahmacārīnaṁ, aṭṭhānametaṁ anavakāso yaṁ so ekādasannaṁ byasanānaṁ aññataraṁ byasanaṁ na nigaccheyya.
root_text.an11.6:4.12: kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati—
root_text.an11.6:4.13: yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkosako paribhāsako ariyūpavādo sabrahmacārīnaṁ, aṭṭhānametaṁ anavakāso yaṁ so imesaṁ ekādasannaṁ byasanānaṁ aññataraṁ byasanaṁ na nigaccheyyā”ti.

translation_text.an11.6:1.1: “Mendicants, any mendicant who abuses and insults their spiritual companions, denouncing the noble ones, will, without a doubt, fall into one or other of these eleven disasters.
translation_text.an11.6:2.3: What they have achieved falls away.
translation_text.an11.6:2.4: They don’t refine their good qualities.
translation_text.an11.6:2.5: Or they overestimate their good qualities.
translation_text.an11.6:2.6: Or they lead the spiritual life dissatisfied.
translation_text.an11.6:2.7: Or they commit a corrupt offense.
translation_text.an11.6:2.8: Or they resign the training and return to a lesser life.
translation_text.an11.6:2.9: Or they contract a severe illness.
translation_text.an11.6:2.10: Or they go mad or lose their mind.
translation_text.an11.6:2.11: They feel lost when they die.
translation_text.an11.6:2.12: And when their body breaks up, after death, they are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an11.6:2.13: Any mendicant who abuses and insults their spiritual companions, denouncing the noble ones, will, without a doubt, fall into one or other of these eleven disasters.
translation_text.an11.6:3.1: Any mendicant who does not abuse and insult their spiritual companions, denouncing the noble ones, will, without a doubt, not fall into one or other of these eleven disasters.
translation_text.an11.6:4.3: What they have achieved falls away.
translation_text.an11.6:4.4: They don’t refine their good qualities.
translation_text.an11.6:4.6: Or they lead the spiritual life dissatisfied.
translation_text.an11.6:4.7: Or they commit one of the corrupt offenses.
translation_text.an11.6:4.8: Or they resign the training and return to a lesser life.
translation_text.an11.6:4.9: Or they contract a severe illness.
translation_text.an11.6:4.10: Or they go mad or lose their mind.
translation_text.an11.6:4.11: They feel lost when they die.
translation_text.an11.6:4.12: And when their body breaks up, after death, they are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an11.6:4.13: Any mendicant who does not abuse and insult their spiritual companions, denouncing the noble ones, will, without a doubt, not fall into one or other of these eleven disasters.”

variant_text.an11.6:1.1: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkosako … → etthantare pāṭho si, sya-all, km, pts1ed potthakesu na
